title: aristolochia lingua var. acuminata
intro: Aristolochia lingua var. acuminata, also known as the tongue snakeroot or Chinese snake root, is a plant species within the Aristolochia genus, characterized by its unique, tongue-shaped leaves and twining vines. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to the presence of aristolochic acids. The health benefits of Aristolochia lingua var. acuminata include the potential treatment of fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antibacterial agent. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be approached with caution due to potential toxicity and side effects.
